title: 9Btfi30523Gn7G706Vxllwlff9Eobc
summary:
China's crude imports saw a reversal in year-on-year growth in November, primarily dragged by sluggish Shandong demand. This also puts China's discounted oil imports in check. By Emma Li
The PMI (Purchasing Managers' Index) for China dipped to 49.3 in November, falling below the 50% threshold for the second month.

children.title: China's crude imports saw a reversal in year-on-year growth in November, primarily dragged by sluggish Shandong demand. This also puts China's discounted oil imports in check.

children.section_path_text: China's crude imports saw a reversal in year-on-year growth in November, primarily dragged by sluggish Shandong demand. This also puts China's discounted oil imports in check.
children.summary:
By Emma Li
The PMI (Purchasing Managers' Index) for China dipped to 49.3 in November, falling below the 50% threshold for the second month. The economic contraction has negatively impacted domestic oil demand, leading to China's seaborne crude imports falling below the 10mbd mark in November for the first time since February.
